## touch-browser v0.5.0

This release closes the main enterprise blockers that were still outside the feature surface: contract versioning, telemetry governance defaults, and public-web QA gating.

### Changed

- Raised the shared contract boundary to `1.1.0` so downstream consumers get an explicit migration signal for the split `pageRisk` / `actionRisk` policy model.
- Promoted public-web QA from opt-in local verification to GitHub workflow coverage on `main`, nightly schedule, and standalone release gating.
- Bumped the Rust workspace, CLI runtime, MCP npm package, MCP server descriptor, workflow client metadata, and standalone lifecycle expectations to `0.5.0`.

### Fixed

- Changed CLI telemetry defaults to `redacted`, so persisted events no longer keep full customer URLs or claim payloads unless `TOUCH_BROWSER_TELEMETRY_MODE=full` is explicitly requested.
- Added `TOUCH_BROWSER_TELEMETRY_MODE=off|redacted|full` so operators can disable telemetry entirely or opt into full payload retention intentionally.
- Scrubbed persisted telemetry URLs down to origin-only in the default path and redacted URL-like tokens from failure notes.

### Verification

- Contract validation passed.
- CLI telemetry test coverage passed.
- Public-web QA gate passed for MDN, Chrome Developers, IANA, and the multi-page follow flow.
- Full `touch-browser-cli` test suite passed.
- Lint passed.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/nangman-infra/touch-browser/compare/v0.4.0...v0.5.0
